name: n8n__remote-mcp
description: Official n8n MCP server, served by every n8n instance (Cloud or self-hosted) at /mcp-server/http. Lets AI agents search, build, test, publish, and execute workflows, inspect executions, list credentials, and manage data tables.
instructions:
**⚠️ Replace the server URL with your instance's MCP endpoint.** There is no central n8n MCP server — every n8n instance (Cloud or self-hosted) serves its own at `https://<your-n8n-domain>/mcp-server/http`. The pre-filled URL is a placeholder and will not connect; copy the real one from **Settings → Instance-level MCP → Connection details** in your n8n instance.

**⚠️ Workflows stay hidden until opted in.** After connecting, `search_workflows` only returns workflows individually marked **Available in MCP**. An empty workflow list usually means nothing is opted in — not a broken connection.

1. In n8n, open **Settings → Instance-level MCP** and toggle **Enable MCP access** (instance owner or admin; requires n8n v2.13+).
2. Open **Connection details**, copy the server URL from the **OAuth** tab, and paste it into this form as the server URL.
3. Mark each workflow the agent should see as **Available in MCP** in the workflow's settings.
4. Connect — no client ID or secret needed. n8n registers the client automatically and redirects you to authorize; revoke access later from the **Connected clients** tab.

Prefer token auth? The **Access Token** tab issues a personal bearer token (`Authorization: Bearer <token>`). Copy it immediately — it is shown only once, and generating a new one revokes the old.

Notes:

- `execute_workflow` runs the published (production) version of a workflow; most other tools also work on unpublished drafts.
- All connected clients see the same set of MCP-enabled workflows (scoped to your user) — there are no per-client restrictions.
- Self-hosted instances can disable MCP entirely via `N8N_DISABLED_MODULES=mcp`.

Docs: https://docs.n8n.io/connect/connect-to-n8n-mcp-server/

tools.name: search_workflows
tools.description: Search for workflows with optional filters; returns a preview of each workflow

tools.name: get_workflow_details
tools.description: Get comprehensive information about a specific workflow, including its triggers

tools.name: execute_workflow
tools.description: Execute a workflow by ID; returns the execution ID immediately without waiting

tools.name: test_workflow
tools.description: Test a workflow using pin data to bypass external services

tools.name: prepare_test_pin_data
tools.description: Generate JSON schemas describing the expected output of nodes that need test data

tools.name: publish_workflow
tools.description: Activate a workflow to make it available for production

tools.name: unpublish_workflow
tools.description: Deactivate a workflow to stop production execution

tools.name: search_projects
tools.description: Find projects accessible to the current user

tools.name: search_folders
tools.description: Locate folders within a project

tools.name: list_tags
tools.description: List all workflow tags in the instance

tools.name: get_execution
tools.description: Get execution details by execution ID and workflow ID

tools.name: search_executions
tools.description: Find workflow executions with optional filters

tools.name: list_credentials
tools.description: List credentials the current user can access

tools.name: get_sdk_reference
tools.description: Access n8n Workflow SDK reference documentation

tools.name: search_nodes
tools.description: Find n8n nodes by service, trigger type, or utility function

tools.name: get_node_types
tools.description: Get TypeScript type definitions for n8n nodes

tools.name: get_workflow_best_practices
tools.description: Retrieve best-practices guidance for workflow techniques

tools.name: explore_node_resources
tools.description: Resolve real values for node resource locators and dropdowns

tools.name: validate_workflow
tools.description: Validate n8n Workflow SDK code by parsing it into a workflow

tools.name: validate_node_config
tools.description: Validate a node configuration independently against its schema

tools.name: create_workflow_from_code
tools.description: Create a workflow in n8n from validated SDK code

tools.name: update_workflow
tools.description: Update an existing workflow by applying targeted partial updates

tools.name: archive_workflow
tools.description: Archive a workflow in n8n by its ID

tools.name: search_data_tables
tools.description: Find data tables accessible to the current user

tools.name: create_data_table
tools.description: Create a new data table with the specified columns

tools.name: add_data_table_column
tools.description: Add a new column to an existing data table

tools.name: rename_data_table_column
tools.description: Rename a column within a data table

tools.name: delete_data_table_column
tools.description: Delete a column from a data table

tools.name: rename_data_table
tools.description: Rename an existing data table

tools.name: add_data_table_rows
tools.description: Insert rows into an existing data table
